Similar collectible bootstrap names should be audited across builtins family installers and internal builtin metadata.

acceptance.new: Bootstrap descriptor names, builtin metadata names, and realm-owned spec names use permanent atoms or are promoted to permanent during bootstrap. Runtime guest-created strings and property keys continue to use collectible atomization where appropriate. Add a focused test or invariant that realm/bootstrap descriptor atoms remain permanent after bootstrap and after an atom sweep.

description.new: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. description:
Close the RegExp-family runnable failures and RegExp-related built-ins skips from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows 674 failures under `built-ins/RegExp` plus 17 `RegExpStringIteratorPrototype` failures.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/RegExp/property-escapes/generated`: 441 TypeErrors.
- `RegExp.prototype[@@replace]`: 67 failures across TypeError and Test262Error outcomes.
- `RegExp.prototype[@@match]`: 45 failures.
- `RegExp.prototype[@@split]`: 43 failures.
- `RegExp.prototype[@@matchAll]`: 23 failures; `@@search`: 21 failures.
- Skip backlog includes `regexp-v-flag` 187, `regexp-modifiers` 147, and `regexp-duplicate-named-groups` 15.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/RegExp/property-escapes/generated/ASCII_Hex_Digit.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/RegExp/prototype/Symbol.replace/arg-1-coerce.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/RegExpStringIteratorPrototype/next/custom-regexpexec-call-throws.js: runtime error: TypeError`

Likely owner areas: `crates/lyng-js/objects/src/regexp.rs`, RegExp builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, parser RegExp validation/lowering, and shared string/iterator operations.
acceptance:
- Focused verification starts with `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins/RegExp --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins-regexp.md -j 4` plus `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins/RegExpStringIteratorPrototype --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins-regexp-iterator.md -j 4`.
- RegExp focused reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected RegExp built-ins tests.
- Stage 3+ RegExp feature skips selected by current policy are implemented or deliberately moved out of selection only with explicit scope justification.
- Unit/integration tests cover any shared RegExp matcher, replacement, split, matchAll, or Unicode-property logic changed.

title: Close remaining Temporal built-in conformance tail
Complete Annex B support in Lyng JS
description:
Lyng JS keeps Annex B inside core ECMA-262 completion and active 6H work. The checked-in reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md currently shows annexB at 1377 selected, 1305 runnable, 225 passed, 1080 failed, 72 skipped, and 16.34% selected pass rate.

No direct dcat tracker exists for Annex B. Existing search only finds an incidental String.prototype.substr note on Temporal issue lyng-55no.

Track closure of Annex B language, parser/runtime, and builtin behavior against Test262. Known current clusters include annexB/language/eval-code/direct and annexB/language/eval-code/indirect SyntaxError failures, annexB/language/global-code and annexB/language/function-code MissingDeclarationBinding lowering failures, and annexB/built-ins/unescape ReferenceError failures. Existing partial support includes lexer HTML-like comments in crates/lyng-js/lexer/src/lexer.rs and parser Annex B call-assignment-target handling in crates/lyng-js/parser/src/parser.rs.

Out of scope: ECMA-402 Intl, web or Node host APIs, and unrelated proposal work.
acceptance: cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter annexB --report /tmp/lyng-js-test262-annexb.md -j 4 reports Failed: 0, Skipped: 0, and Panicked: 0, or every remaining Annex B case is explicitly justified in the checked-in report/manifest flow. Parser, sema, compiler, env, VM, ops, and builtin work stays in the owning crates and is traceable to ECMA-262 Annex B. docs/lyng-js/ecma262-completion.md and reports/js/lyng-js/ agree with the final Annex B status.

title: Eliminate Annex B skips and align reports/docs
Close Annex B frontend and early-error syntax gaps
description:
Close the Annex B frontend and early-error syntax gap for Lyng JS.

Scope includes HTML-like comments, legacy numeric and string literal forms, legacy escape handling, Annex B expression grammar, script-versus-module behavior, and strict/sloppy early-error differences. Existing partial support includes HTML-like comments in crates/lyng-js/lexer/src/lexer.rs and Annex B call-assignment-target handling in crates/lyng-js/parser/src/parser.rs, so new work should extend those ownership points rather than duplicating syntax policy elsewhere.

Baseline context comes from reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md, where the annexB category currently has 1377 selected, 1305 runnable, 225 passed, 1080 failed, and 72 skipped.
acceptance: cargo test -p lyng-js-parser passes. Targeted Test262 verification covers annexB/language/comments, annexB/language/literals, and relevant annexB/language/expressions filters. Strict/sloppy and script/module behavior is covered by parser or sema tests where the behavior is owned before runtime execution.

Close Annex B legacy built-in surface
description:
Close the Annex B legacy builtin surface for Lyng JS.

Scope includes escape, unescape, legacy Date, String, Object, Function, and RegExp surfaces, their property descriptors, and shared abstract-operation/object behavior needed by those builtins. The current report highlights annexB/built-ins/unescape ReferenceError failures, and related legacy builtin gaps may be distributed across annexB/built-ins subdirectories.

Keep builtin installation and dispatch in crates/lyng-js/builtins, proxy-observable object behavior in crates/lyng-js/ops and crates/lyng-js/objects, and avoid duplicating abstract operations inside individual builtin handlers.

Implement Annex B block-level function declaration semantics
description:
Implement Annex B block-level function declaration semantics for global and function code.

Scope includes ECMA-262 Annex B.3.3-style behavior for sloppy-mode block function declarations, declaration collection, binding creation and initialization, and interactions with lexical/var/function declarations. This task owns the current annexB/language/global-code and annexB/language/function-code MissingDeclarationBinding lowering failures shown in reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md.

Keep work in the owning crates: sema for declaration/binding analysis, compiler for lowering, env for environment behavior, and VM for execution semantics. Do not hide binding policy inside unrelated builtin or harness code.

description:

Scope includes Annex B eval declaration-instantiation behavior, sloppy-mode block function declarations inside eval code, direct versus indirect eval environment differences, and interactions with existing dynamic-compilation and environment ownership. This task owns the current annexB/language/eval-code/direct and annexB/language/eval-code/indirect SyntaxError, ReferenceError, and Test262 assertion clusters visible in reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md.

Use the existing dynamic compilation pipeline and environment model. Do not introduce a second eval path or ad hoc VM-only declaration-instantiation policy.
acceptance: Targeted Test262 verification for annexB/language/eval-code/direct and annexB/language/eval-code/indirect reports Failed: 0 and Panicked: 0, with any remaining skips explicitly justified. Focused env/compiler/VM tests cover direct and indirect eval declaration-instantiation behavior touched by the implementation.

title: Implement Annex B direct and indirect eval semantics
Close remaining Temporal built-in conformance tail
description:
Close the non-Intl Temporal built-ins conformance tail from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows 411 failures under `built-ins/Temporal`.

Major clusters:
- `Temporal/ZonedDateTime`: 158 failures, including 111 Test262Error and 37 RangeError outcomes.
- `Temporal/Duration`: 99 failures, including `Duration.prototype.round` RangeError/Test262Error clusters.
- `Temporal/PlainDateTime`: 79 failures across `since`, `until`, rounding, leap-second, and relativeTo behavior.
- `Temporal/PlainYearMonth`: 29 failures; `Temporal/PlainDate`: 25 failures.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/Temporal/Duration/prototype/round/balances-up-to-weeks.js: runtime error: RangeError`
- `built-ins/Temporal/ZonedDateTime/from/argument-propertybag-offset-not-agreeing-with-timezone.js: runtime error: Test262Error`
- `built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime/prototype/since/round-relative-to-receiver.js: runtime error: RangeError`

Likely owner areas: `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs`, `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs`, `crates/lyng-js/ops/src/temporal.rs`, `crates/lyng-js/objects/src/temporal.rs`, and `docs/lyng-js/temporal-support-matrix.md` if support status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification starts with `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins/Temporal --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins-temporal.md -j 4`.
- The focused Temporal report has `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected non-Intl Temporal built-ins tests.
- Existing `cargo test -p lyng-js-tests temporal` coverage passes, and new direct tests cover changed Temporal abstract operations.
- Temporal support docs stay aligned if support boundaries or known limitations change.
notes: Implemented Temporal.ZonedDateTime.prototype.getTimeZoneTransition for UTC/fixed-offset zones; added Annex B String.prototype.substr used by Temporal Test262 helpers; corrected Temporal Instant epoch rounding to use RoundNumberToIncrementAsIfPositive while keeping signed duration/difference rounding separate. Continued work added Temporal Duration float64-representable field semantics for Instant/PlainDateTime/ZonedDateTime difference output, Duration.add/subtract rebalancing with operand largest-unit hints, and RangeError rejection for Duration.add/subtract operands with nonzero calendar units. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-ops nanosecond_rounding => 2 passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_supports_annex_b_string_substr => passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests temporal => 168 passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-types => 26 passed; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-ops -p lyng-js-builtins --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic => completed with existing warnings; targeted Duration add/subtract precision Test262 slices => 8 passed, 0 failed across add/subtract argument-duration-precision-exact-numerical-values and precision-exact-mathematical-values; Duration add/subtract no-calendar-units => 4 passed, 0 failed; Instant since/until float64-representable-integer => 4 passed, 0 failed; PlainDateTime since/until float64-representable-integer => 4 passed, 0 failed; ZonedDateTime since/until float64-representable-integer => 4 passed, 0 failed; Test262 getTimeZoneTransition => 28 passed, 0 failed; Instant round => 82 passed, 0 failed; Instant toString => 108 passed, 0 failed; latest full built-ins/Temporal slice => 8426 passed, 726 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked (previous local checkpoint was 8392 passed, 760 failed; issue-start local baseline was 8340 passed, 812 failed). Known blocker found while investigating Instant largestUnit/smallestUnit mismatch: a loop lexical-capture/arrow closure substrate bug, reproducible outside Temporal with a for-loop binding captured by an arrow closure, still causes the Instant mismatch Test262 files to fail with TypeError. Result-out-of-range-3 for Duration.add/subtract still fails because it needs preserving Duration operand original largest-unit/internal duration shape across Temporal.Duration.from objects. Top remaining clusters: Duration.prototype.round RangeError (80), ZonedDateTime.from Test262Error (42), ZonedDateTime.prototype.with Test262Error (40), PlainDateTime since/until RangeError (32 each), Duration.prototype.total Test262Error (30).

title: Close remaining Temporal built-in conformance tail
Complete Annex B support in Lyng JS
description:
Lyng JS keeps Annex B inside core ECMA-262 completion and active 6H work. The checked-in reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md currently shows annexB at 1377 selected, 1305 runnable, 225 passed, 1080 failed, 72 skipped, and 16.34% selected pass rate.

No direct dcat tracker exists for Annex B. Existing search only finds an incidental String.prototype.substr note on Temporal issue lyng-55no.

Track closure of Annex B language, parser/runtime, and builtin behavior against Test262. Known current clusters include annexB/language/eval-code/direct and annexB/language/eval-code/indirect SyntaxError failures, annexB/language/global-code and annexB/language/function-code MissingDeclarationBinding lowering failures, and annexB/built-ins/unescape ReferenceError failures. Existing partial support includes lexer HTML-like comments in crates/lyng-js/lexer/src/lexer.rs and parser Annex B call-assignment-target handling in crates/lyng-js/parser/src/parser.rs.

Out of scope: ECMA-402 Intl, web or Node host APIs, and unrelated proposal work.
acceptance: cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter annexB --report /tmp/lyng-js-test262-annexb.md -j 4 reports Failed: 0, Skipped: 0, and Panicked: 0, or every remaining Annex B case is explicitly justified in the checked-in report/manifest flow. Parser, sema, compiler, env, VM, ops, and builtin work stays in the owning crates and is traceable to ECMA-262 Annex B. docs/lyng-js/ecma262-completion.md and reports/js/lyng-js/ agree with the final Annex B status.

title: Complete Annex B support in Lyng JS
Close Annex B legacy built-in surface
description:
Close the Annex B legacy builtin surface for Lyng JS.

Scope includes escape, unescape, legacy Date, String, Object, Function, and RegExp surfaces, their property descriptors, and shared abstract-operation/object behavior needed by those builtins. The current report highlights annexB/built-ins/unescape ReferenceError failures, and related legacy builtin gaps may be distributed across annexB/built-ins subdirectories.

Keep builtin installation and dispatch in crates/lyng-js/builtins, proxy-observable object behavior in crates/lyng-js/ops and crates/lyng-js/objects, and avoid duplicating abstract operations inside individual builtin handlers.
acceptance: cargo test -p lyng-js-tests passes. Targeted Test262 verification for annexB/built-ins reports Failed: 0 and Panicked: 0, with descriptor/name/length behavior covered for added legacy builtins. Shared object or abstract-operation changes include focused tests in the owning layer.

title: Close Annex B legacy built-in surface
Implement Annex B block-level function declaration semantics
description:
Implement Annex B block-level function declaration semantics for global and function code.

Scope includes ECMA-262 Annex B.3.3-style behavior for sloppy-mode block function declarations, declaration collection, binding creation and initialization, and interactions with lexical/var/function declarations. This task owns the current annexB/language/global-code and annexB/language/function-code MissingDeclarationBinding lowering failures shown in reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md.

Keep work in the owning crates: sema for declaration/binding analysis, compiler for lowering, env for environment behavior, and VM for execution semantics. Do not hide binding policy inside unrelated builtin or harness code.

title: Implement Annex B direct and indirect eval semantics
Close Annex B frontend and early-error syntax gaps
description:
Close the Annex B frontend and early-error syntax gap for Lyng JS.

Scope includes HTML-like comments, legacy numeric and string literal forms, legacy escape handling, Annex B expression grammar, script-versus-module behavior, and strict/sloppy early-error differences. Existing partial support includes HTML-like comments in crates/lyng-js/lexer/src/lexer.rs and Annex B call-assignment-target handling in crates/lyng-js/parser/src/parser.rs, so new work should extend those ownership points rather than duplicating syntax policy elsewhere.

Baseline context comes from reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md, where the annexB category currently has 1377 selected, 1305 runnable, 225 passed, 1080 failed, and 72 skipped.
acceptance: cargo test -p lyng-js-parser passes. Targeted Test262 verification covers annexB/language/comments, annexB/language/literals, and relevant annexB/language/expressions filters. Strict/sloppy and script/module behavior is covered by parser or sema tests where the behavior is owned before runtime execution.

Burn down remaining Test262 language failures and skips to 100%
description:
Own the full raw Test262 `language` gap for Lyng JS, including both in-scope ECMA-262 failures and currently skipped proposal coverage. This epic tracks the remaining work needed to move the language slice from the April 23, 2026 baseline to zero unexplained failures and zero skips.

Baseline evidence:
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` produced 23,637 selected, 22,768 runnable, 22,005 passed, 763 failed, 869 skipped.
- A repeat `--list-failures` run reported 761 failures, so there is minor run-to-run variance; treat the April 23, 2026 /tmp artifacts as the analysis source of truth.

This epic intentionally includes proposal-driven skips under `language`, not just Phase 6 core semantics, because the goal is to clear the raw language pass-rate gap to 100%.
acceptance:
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` reports `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0`.
- Child issues cover the main failure and skip clusters with explicit acceptance criteria and relevant verification commands.
- Tracker verification via `dcat list --agent-only` and `dcat list <epic-id>` shows one epic and the full child breakdown.
notes: Use the /tmp April 23, 2026 language artifacts and the checked-in Test262 harness/reporting flow as the baseline. No dependencies should be added between child issues unless implementation sequencing later proves necessary.

title: Burn down remaining Test262 language failures and skips to 100%
Add --shell print global to lyng-js CLI
description: lyng-js currently keeps the CLI embedding spec-only and does not expose harness, browser, or Node-style globals. The V8/v8-v7 benchmark in /Users/sondre/dev/js-engine-benchmark uses print(...) and only falls back to console.log, so lyng-js cannot run it today. Add an opt-in --shell CLI flag that installs a print(...) global while preserving default strict behavior.
acceptance: cargo test -p lyng-js-cli passes, and a release build can run the documented print smoke checks.

title: Add --shell print global to lyng-js CLI
Add --shell print global to lyng-js CLI
description: lyng-js currently keeps the CLI embedding spec-only and does not expose harness, browser, or Node-style globals. The V8/v8-v7 benchmark in /Users/sondre/dev/js-engine-benchmark uses print(...) and only falls back to console.log, so lyng-js cannot run it today. Add an opt-in --shell CLI flag that installs a print(...) global while preserving default strict behavior.
acceptance: Default lyng-js <script> still leaves print undefined and reports ReferenceError for print(1+1). lyng-js --shell <script> installs global print(...), converts each argument through VM string conversion, joins arguments with spaces, writes a trailing newline to stdout, and exits successfully. --shell is accepted only before the script path; trailing --shell after the script path is rejected as an unexpected trailing argument. Script and module execution paths use the shell extension only when shell mode is enabled. cargo test -p lyng-js-cli passes, and a release build can run the documented print smoke checks.
notes: Implementation scope: add crates/lyng-js/cli/src/extensions.rs with a minimal CliRealmExtension provider, wire shell_mode through CliInvocation and parse_args, and route script/module execution through the VM extension entry points only when --shell is present.

notes.new: Implementation scope: add crates/lyng-js/cli/src/extensions.rs with a minimal CliRealmExtension provider, wire shell_mode through CliInvocation and parse_args, and route script/module execution through the VM extension entry points only when --shell is present.

title: Add --shell print global to lyng-js CLI
Add --shell print global to lyng-js CLI
description: lyng-js currently keeps the CLI embedding spec-only and does not expose harness, browser, or Node-style globals. The V8/v8-v7 benchmark in /Users/sondre/dev/js-engine-benchmark uses print(...) and only falls back to console.log, so lyng-js cannot run it today. Add an opt-in --shell CLI flag that installs a print(...) global while preserving default strict behavior.

title: Add --shell print global to lyng-js CLI
Burn down remaining Test262 language failures and skips to 100%
description:
Own the full raw Test262 `language` gap for Lyng JS, including both in-scope ECMA-262 failures and currently skipped proposal coverage. This epic tracks the remaining work needed to move the language slice from the April 23, 2026 baseline to zero unexplained failures and zero skips.

Baseline evidence:
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` produced 23,637 selected, 22,768 runnable, 22,005 passed, 763 failed, 869 skipped.
- A repeat `--list-failures` run reported 761 failures, so there is minor run-to-run variance; treat the April 23, 2026 /tmp artifacts as the analysis source of truth.

This epic intentionally includes proposal-driven skips under `language`, not just Phase 6 core semantics, because the goal is to clear the raw language pass-rate gap to 100%.
acceptance:
- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter language --report /tmp/lyng-js-language.md -j 4` reports `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0`.
- Child issues cover the main failure and skip clusters with explicit acceptance criteria and relevant verification commands.
- Tracker verification via `dcat list --agent-only` and `dcat list <epic-id>` shows one epic and the full child breakdown.
notes: Use the /tmp April 23, 2026 language artifacts and the checked-in Test262 harness/reporting flow as the baseline. No dependencies should be added between child issues unless implementation sequencing later proves necessary.

Implement remaining Stage 3+ built-in feature skips
description:
Implement selected Stage 3+ built-in feature skips that currently count against the Test262 built-ins selected pass rate.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- The runner selects Stage 3+ proposals by default; below-Stage-3 proposal exclusions are out of scope for this issue.

Skip clusters in scope:
- iterator-helpers: 503 skips.
- set-methods: 186 skips.
- Array.fromAsync: 95 skips.
- upsert: 66 skips.
- symbols-as-weakmap-keys: 26 skips.
- Atomics.waitAsync and Atomics.pause: 107 combined skips.
- Promise.try and Promise.withResolvers: 18 combined skips.
- Error.isError: 12 skips.

Relevant source owners: iterator and collection builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins`, iterator/promise/shared-memory abstract operations in `crates/lyng-js/ops`, weak collection object semantics in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, and skip policy in `tools/lyng-js-test262` only when the feature-selection policy itself changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes selected filters for `built-ins/Iterator`, `built-ins/AsyncIteratorPrototype`, `built-ins/Set`, `built-ins/Map`, `built-ins/WeakMap`, `built-ins/Array/fromAsync`, `built-ins/Atomics`, `built-ins/Promise`, and `built-ins/Error`.
- All Stage 3+ built-in feature skips selected by the current runner policy are either implemented and passing or explicitly removed from selection only because the project policy changes.
- Whole `built-ins` report skip count drops by the covered feature counts without introducing new runnable failures.
- New implementations use existing iterator, promise/job, collection, weak-key, and shared-memory ownership boundaries.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Updated PR https://github.com/lyngsys/lyng/pull/2 with three more clusters:

- set-methods (was 186 skips → 712/712 Set.prototype tests pass)
- upsert (was 66 skips → 142/142 Map+WeakMap getOrInsert(Computed) tests pass)
- symbols-as-weakmap-keys (was 26 skips, engine already supported symbols as WeakHeapRef; only the test262 feature gate needed removing)

Combined PR now covers 6 of 8 clusters (Error.isError, Promise.try, Promise.withResolvers, set-methods, upsert, symbols-as-weakmap-keys).

Still on the issue:
- iterator-helpers (503 skips) — largest, requires full Iterator prototype helpers (map/filter/reduce/take/drop/flatMap/forEach/some/every/find/toArray/zip/zipKeyed/concat).
- Atomics.waitAsync + Atomics.pause (107 skips) — needs async wait infra.
- Array.fromAsync (95 skips) — partial work in commit a71e9c9.

Worth flagging for the next session:
- symmetricDifference's spec checks SetDataHas against the LIVE receiver while keeping a snapshot of the receiver as the result base. The mutation tests rely on this distinction.
- isSubsetOf/isDisjointFrom small-this branches must iterate O.[[SetData]] by live index, not via a snapshot, so callback-driven mutations on baseSet are observed.
- iterator-close on early exit must NOT pre-set done=true; iterator_close itself decides whether to call return() based on the done flag.
- The post-callback recheck pattern in Map.getOrInsertComputed lets a callback's own set() be overwritten by the returned value; the canonical weak_map_set already acts as upsert so WeakMap.getOrInsertComputed doesn't need an explicit recheck.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Update on remaining clusters:

DONE in this session (commit 53926b1):
- Atomics.pause: 12/12 pass (new builtin, simple validation + return undefined).
- Atomics.waitAsync: 94/94 runnable pass (was 92/94). Engine impl already existed; main work was removing the test262 feature gate and fixing waitAsync to always park even when a timeout was supplied (the previous eager 'timed-out' fulfillment broke synchronous-notify tests). 108 of 202 selected tests are still skipped because they use $262.agent.start multi-agent harness which we don't provide.

DONE in prior commits (already on branch):
- Array.fromAsync: 186/186 pass. Re-measurement on the worktree shows the cluster is fully closed by commit a71e9c9 (the '95 skips' baseline figure was stale). No further work needed.

Net summary so far: 7 of 8 clusters closed in PR #2. Whole built-ins run now: 40879 pass / 2620 fail / 2976 skip / 87.96% (was 40215 / 2624 / 3636 / 86.53%).

REMAINING: iterator-helpers (~503 skips). I scoped this in detail; full implementation is multi-day and I am stopping here rather than landing partial work. Detailed plan follows so the next session can pick it up cold.

== iterator-helpers implementation plan ==

Current engine state:
- No global Iterator constructor or Iterator.prototype.constructor.
- Iterator.prototype currently has only [Symbol.iterator] (the built-in identity %IteratorPrototype% from ECMA-262 §27.1.4).
- testdata/test262/test/built-ins/Iterator/{prototype,from,zip,zipKeyed,concat} contains 1020 selected tests; only 22 are runnable today (10 pass, 12 fail), gated by 'iterator-helpers' in tools/lyng-js-test262/src/selection.rs.
- Generator infra exists (lyng-js/objects/src/functions.rs has Generator object kind and GeneratorState enum), but iterator helpers need their own state machines, not generator suspension.

Scope in spec order:
A. Iterator constructor (sec-iterator-constructor)
   - new Iterator() throws TypeError if called directly with no NewTarget != Iterator (subclassable only).
   - Needs new BuiltinIntrinsic::Iterator + bootstrap allocation.
B. Iterator.from(O) (sec-iterator.from)
   - If GetIteratorFlattenable(O, reject-strings) returns an iterator already inheriting from Iterator.prototype, return it.
   - Otherwise wrap in WrapForValidIteratorPrototype.
   - Needs WrapForValidIteratorPrototype intrinsic + its [[Iterated]] internal slot.
C. Iterator.prototype non-method props
   - constructor (writable, configurable) → Iterator constructor
   - [Symbol.toStringTag] = 'Iterator'
D. Eager helpers (return concrete value, no helper iterator needed):
   - reduce, forEach, some, every, find, toArray
   - These are straightforward: GetIteratorDirect(this), iterate, apply callback, return result.
E. Lazy helpers (return new IteratorHelper object that wraps state):
   - map, filter, take, drop, flatMap
   - Each needs a new IteratorHelper object kind storing { underlying iterator, callback (or limit/skip), inner-iterator state for flatMap, done flag, counter for take/drop }. Their [[next]] applies the transformation lazily.
   - Per spec each is a generator that yields lazily. Cleanest implementation: introduce ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper(record) with a discriminant for the kind, then a single iterator_helper_next_builtin that switches on the kind.
F. New Stage-3 statics: Iterator.zip, Iterator.zipKeyed, Iterator.concat
   - Each returns a new IteratorHelper. Heavy lifting in the GetSetRecord-like input validation.

Implementation order I would recommend:
1. Land the Iterator constructor + Iterator.prototype.constructor + [Symbol.toStringTag] + the 6 eager helpers + Iterator.from. This alone closes a substantial portion of tests (toArray's 18 tests, reduce's 32, etc.) without needing a new ObjectKind. Estimate: ~250-300 LOC in dispatch + the usual builtin-id + family install + descriptor + metadata wiring.
2. Add ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper { iterated, kind, state } with a single iterator_helper_next_builtin dispatched on kind. Implement map / filter / take / drop / flatMap. Their static prototype is a fresh object inheriting from Iterator.prototype; iterating it advances the state.
3. Implement Iterator.zip / zipKeyed / concat using the same IteratorHelper machinery.
4. Land a final commit removing 'iterator-helpers' from UNSUPPORTED_FEATURES.

Builtin ID space: next free after this PR is 3_542. Reserve a contiguous block of ~16-18 IDs (Iterator constructor, Iterator.from, 11 prototype methods, zip/zipKeyed/concat, and the iterator_helper_next dispatcher). Update CORE_BUILTIN_NAMESPACE_END or COMPLETION_BUILTIN_NAMESPACE_END accordingly.

Files to touch (most existing):
- crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs + builtin_ids/core_ids/iterators.rs (or a new module)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/descriptors.rs (add BuiltinIntrinsic::Iterator)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap/descriptors.rs (allocate Iterator intrinsic)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/families/{iterators,mod,installed}.rs
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/iterators.rs (already exists)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata/core/language.rs (or a new file)
- crates/lyng-js/objects/src/functions.rs (new ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper)
- crates/lyng-js/objects/src/core.rs (possibly OrdinaryObjectData::IteratorHelper)
- tools/lyng-js-test262/src/selection.rs (final gate removal)

Risk areas:
- Iterator.from's GetIteratorFlattenable abstract operation interacts with the existing iterator pipeline; needs care with primitive (string) rejection vs acceptance.
- flatMap needs to track an inner iterator and recursively pull from it before pulling more from the outer, with proper iterator-close on early exit.
- Subclassability of Iterator (extends Iterator) — the constructor needs to allow GetPrototypeFromConstructor(NewTarget, '%Iterator.prototype%') so classes like 'class MyIter extends Iterator { ... }' work; many tests rely on this.
- Helper iterators must close the underlying iterator on early exit (e.g. take(0), filter throws) — same iterator_close pattern used in set-methods is the playbook.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Stage 1 iterator-helpers landed in commit 03c8e28 on PR #2:

Implemented:
- Iterator global constructor (subclass-only, throws on direct call/new without subclass)
- Iterator.prototype[Symbol.iterator]
- Iterator.prototype.constructor and Iterator.prototype[Symbol.toStringTag] as the brand-checked accessor pairs the spec requires
- Iterator.prototype.reduce / forEach / some / every / find / toArray (all spec-faithful incl. early-close, argument-validation-before-GetIteratorDirect, counter semantics)
- Iterator.from fast path (returns input as-is when its prototype chain includes %Iterator.prototype%)

Per-method test262 results:
- reduce: 60/60
- forEach: 54/54
- some: 66/66
- every: 66/66
- find: 64/64
- toArray: 36/36
- constructor accessor: 4/4
- Symbol.toStringTag accessor: 4/4
- Symbol.iterator: 4/4
- Iterator constructor / proto / subclassable / length / name / prop-desc: 12/12

Iterator.from is at 14/38; the 24 failures all require WrapForValidIteratorPrototype which I deferred along with the lazy helpers.

Whole built-ins: 41,303 pass / 2,982 fail / 2,190 skip / 88.87% selected pass rate (was 87.96%, +0.91pp).

DEFERRED to Stage 2 (still on this issue):
- Iterator.prototype.{map, filter, take, drop, flatMap}
- Iterator.from wrap-iterator branch (WrapForValidIteratorPrototype)
- Iterator.{zip, zipKeyed, concat}

Stage 2 implementation plan (refines lyng-3k8k-c3):
1. Add ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper carrying { iterated: ObjectRef, kind: u8 discriminant, callback_or_limit: Value, counter: u32 } in named slots. Already-existing ObjectColdData enum is in crates/lyng-js/objects/src/functions.rs around the GeneratorObject sites. The state can fit in 4 named slots with no new payload type if we encode kind as a small enum.
2. New iterator_helper_next_builtin dispatching on the kind to advance the underlying iterator and apply the transformation, plus iterator_helper_return_builtin to forward return() to the underlying iterator.
3. Map / filter / take / drop / flatMap each construct an IteratorHelper with their own kind. flatMap stores an inner-iterator ObjectRef in an additional slot.
4. Iterator.from's wrap branch builds a WrapForValidIteratorPrototype-style helper too, holding the wrapped { next, return } pair.
5. Iterator.zip / zipKeyed / concat: same machinery; zip/zipKeyed allocate Vec<IteratorRecord> equivalent and pull from each on every step.

The dispatch pattern from set-methods (live receiver, snapshot, iterator_close on early exit) carries over directly. Risk is mostly in flatMap inner-iterator state machine and getting the iterator_close ordering right when an outer iterator throws while an inner is mid-flight.

8 of 8 clusters now have at least partial coverage in PR #2. Full closure of lyng-3k8k requires Stage 2 as the next session.

Update on remaining clusters:

DONE in this session (commit 53926b1):
- Atomics.pause: 12/12 pass (new builtin, simple validation + return undefined).
- Atomics.waitAsync: 94/94 runnable pass (was 92/94). Engine impl already existed; main work was removing the test262 feature gate and fixing waitAsync to always park even when a timeout was supplied (the previous eager 'timed-out' fulfillment broke synchronous-notify tests). 108 of 202 selected tests are still skipped because they use $262.agent.start multi-agent harness which we don't provide.

DONE in prior commits (already on branch):
- Array.fromAsync: 186/186 pass. Re-measurement on the worktree shows the cluster is fully closed by commit a71e9c9 (the '95 skips' baseline figure was stale). No further work needed.

Net summary so far: 7 of 8 clusters closed in PR #2. Whole built-ins run now: 40879 pass / 2620 fail / 2976 skip / 87.96% (was 40215 / 2624 / 3636 / 86.53%).

REMAINING: iterator-helpers (~503 skips). I scoped this in detail; full implementation is multi-day and I am stopping here rather than landing partial work. Detailed plan follows so the next session can pick it up cold.

== iterator-helpers implementation plan ==

Current engine state:
- No global Iterator constructor or Iterator.prototype.constructor.
- Iterator.prototype currently has only [Symbol.iterator] (the built-in identity %IteratorPrototype% from ECMA-262 §27.1.4).
- testdata/test262/test/built-ins/Iterator/{prototype,from,zip,zipKeyed,concat} contains 1020 selected tests; only 22 are runnable today (10 pass, 12 fail), gated by 'iterator-helpers' in tools/lyng-js-test262/src/selection.rs.
- Generator infra exists (lyng-js/objects/src/functions.rs has Generator object kind and GeneratorState enum), but iterator helpers need their own state machines, not generator suspension.

Scope in spec order:
A. Iterator constructor (sec-iterator-constructor)
   - new Iterator() throws TypeError if called directly with no NewTarget != Iterator (subclassable only).
   - Needs new BuiltinIntrinsic::Iterator + bootstrap allocation.
B. Iterator.from(O) (sec-iterator.from)
   - If GetIteratorFlattenable(O, reject-strings) returns an iterator already inheriting from Iterator.prototype, return it.
   - Otherwise wrap in WrapForValidIteratorPrototype.
   - Needs WrapForValidIteratorPrototype intrinsic + its [[Iterated]] internal slot.
C. Iterator.prototype non-method props
   - constructor (writable, configurable) → Iterator constructor
   - [Symbol.toStringTag] = 'Iterator'
D. Eager helpers (return concrete value, no helper iterator needed):
   - reduce, forEach, some, every, find, toArray
   - These are straightforward: GetIteratorDirect(this), iterate, apply callback, return result.
E. Lazy helpers (return new IteratorHelper object that wraps state):
   - map, filter, take, drop, flatMap
   - Each needs a new IteratorHelper object kind storing { underlying iterator, callback (or limit/skip), inner-iterator state for flatMap, done flag, counter for take/drop }. Their [[next]] applies the transformation lazily.
   - Per spec each is a generator that yields lazily. Cleanest implementation: introduce ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper(record) with a discriminant for the kind, then a single iterator_helper_next_builtin that switches on the kind.
F. New Stage-3 statics: Iterator.zip, Iterator.zipKeyed, Iterator.concat
   - Each returns a new IteratorHelper. Heavy lifting in the GetSetRecord-like input validation.

Implementation order I would recommend:
1. Land the Iterator constructor + Iterator.prototype.constructor + [Symbol.toStringTag] + the 6 eager helpers + Iterator.from. This alone closes a substantial portion of tests (toArray's 18 tests, reduce's 32, etc.) without needing a new ObjectKind. Estimate: ~250-300 LOC in dispatch + the usual builtin-id + family install + descriptor + metadata wiring.
2. Add ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper { iterated, kind, state } with a single iterator_helper_next_builtin dispatched on kind. Implement map / filter / take / drop / flatMap. Their static prototype is a fresh object inheriting from Iterator.prototype; iterating it advances the state.
3. Implement Iterator.zip / zipKeyed / concat using the same IteratorHelper machinery.
4. Land a final commit removing 'iterator-helpers' from UNSUPPORTED_FEATURES.

Builtin ID space: next free after this PR is 3_542. Reserve a contiguous block of ~16-18 IDs (Iterator constructor, Iterator.from, 11 prototype methods, zip/zipKeyed/concat, and the iterator_helper_next dispatcher). Update CORE_BUILTIN_NAMESPACE_END or COMPLETION_BUILTIN_NAMESPACE_END accordingly.

Files to touch (most existing):
- crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs + builtin_ids/core_ids/iterators.rs (or a new module)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/descriptors.rs (add BuiltinIntrinsic::Iterator)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap/descriptors.rs (allocate Iterator intrinsic)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/families/{iterators,mod,installed}.rs
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/iterators.rs (already exists)
- crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata/core/language.rs (or a new file)
- crates/lyng-js/objects/src/functions.rs (new ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper)
- crates/lyng-js/objects/src/core.rs (possibly OrdinaryObjectData::IteratorHelper)
- tools/lyng-js-test262/src/selection.rs (final gate removal)

Risk areas:
- Iterator.from's GetIteratorFlattenable abstract operation interacts with the existing iterator pipeline; needs care with primitive (string) rejection vs acceptance.
- flatMap needs to track an inner iterator and recursively pull from it before pulling more from the outer, with proper iterator-close on early exit.
- Subclassability of Iterator (extends Iterator) — the constructor needs to allow GetPrototypeFromConstructor(NewTarget, '%Iterator.prototype%') so classes like 'class MyIter extends Iterator { ... }' work; many tests rely on this.
- Helper iterators must close the underlying iterator on early exit (e.g. take(0), filter throws) — same iterator_close pattern used in set-methods is the playbook.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Stage 1 iterator-helpers landed in commit 03c8e28 on PR #2:

Implemented:
- Iterator global constructor (subclass-only, throws on direct call/new without subclass)
- Iterator.prototype[Symbol.iterator]
- Iterator.prototype.constructor and Iterator.prototype[Symbol.toStringTag] as the brand-checked accessor pairs the spec requires
- Iterator.prototype.reduce / forEach / some / every / find / toArray (all spec-faithful incl. early-close, argument-validation-before-GetIteratorDirect, counter semantics)
- Iterator.from fast path (returns input as-is when its prototype chain includes %Iterator.prototype%)

Per-method test262 results:
- reduce: 60/60
- forEach: 54/54
- some: 66/66
- every: 66/66
- find: 64/64
- toArray: 36/36
- constructor accessor: 4/4
- Symbol.toStringTag accessor: 4/4
- Symbol.iterator: 4/4
- Iterator constructor / proto / subclassable / length / name / prop-desc: 12/12

Iterator.from is at 14/38; the 24 failures all require WrapForValidIteratorPrototype which I deferred along with the lazy helpers.

Whole built-ins: 41,303 pass / 2,982 fail / 2,190 skip / 88.87% selected pass rate (was 87.96%, +0.91pp).

DEFERRED to Stage 2 (still on this issue):
- Iterator.prototype.{map, filter, take, drop, flatMap}
- Iterator.from wrap-iterator branch (WrapForValidIteratorPrototype)
- Iterator.{zip, zipKeyed, concat}

Stage 2 implementation plan (refines lyng-3k8k-c3):
1. Add ObjectColdData::IteratorHelper carrying { iterated: ObjectRef, kind: u8 discriminant, callback_or_limit: Value, counter: u32 } in named slots. Already-existing ObjectColdData enum is in crates/lyng-js/objects/src/functions.rs around the GeneratorObject sites. The state can fit in 4 named slots with no new payload type if we encode kind as a small enum.
2. New iterator_helper_next_builtin dispatching on the kind to advance the underlying iterator and apply the transformation, plus iterator_helper_return_builtin to forward return() to the underlying iterator.
3. Map / filter / take / drop / flatMap each construct an IteratorHelper with their own kind. flatMap stores an inner-iterator ObjectRef in an additional slot.
4. Iterator.from's wrap branch builds a WrapForValidIteratorPrototype-style helper too, holding the wrapped { next, return } pair.
5. Iterator.zip / zipKeyed / concat: same machinery; zip/zipKeyed allocate Vec<IteratorRecord> equivalent and pull from each on every step.

The dispatch pattern from set-methods (live receiver, snapshot, iterator_close on early exit) carries over directly. Risk is mostly in flatMap inner-iterator state machine and getting the iterator_close ordering right when an outer iterator throws while an inner is mid-flight.

8 of 8 clusters now have at least partial coverage in PR #2. Full closure of lyng-3k8k requires Stage 2 as the next session.

title: Close Array and Array.prototype built-in failures
Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor array-like allocation guard slice landed. Root cause: array-like construction collected indexed properties before checking whether AllocateTypedArrayBuffer could fit; excessive lengths timed out and could touch user getters before RangeError. Added a regression proving Uint8Array/BigInt64Array oversize array-like construction throws RangeError before index 0 access. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 object-arg slices passed 28/28 and 31/31 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-object-arg-after-oversize.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-object-arg-after-oversize.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-oversize.md is 653 passed, 83 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: PR opened: https://github.com/lyngsys/lyng/pull/4 — Uint8Array base64/hex (60/60 closed, focused slice now 100%). Branch off main; stacks parallel to PR #3 (DataView BigInt), no expected conflicts. Issue stays in_progress for the remaining clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com

Root causes: explicit undefined length was treated as length 0 instead of absent, and the ArrayBuffer-backed constructor did not recheck detachment after byteOffset/length ToIndex coercion. Added regressions for undefined length modulo behavior and valueOf-triggered detach during byteOffset/length coercion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 buffer-arg slices passed 54/54 and 52/52 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-buffer-arg.md is 661 passed, 75 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. Root cause: TypedArrayCreate helper accepted a custom constructor result whose typed-array length was shorter than the requested length, so from/of wrote past the returned view and produced RangeError instead of the required TypeError. Added a regression covering iterable and array-like from plus of for Uint8Array and BigInt64Array. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; the four custom-ctor Test262 files all passed 1/1 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-bigint-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-short-custom-after.md, and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-bigint-short-custom-after.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-short-custom.md is 665 passed, 71 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: PR opened: https://github.com/lyngsys/lyng/pull/4 — Uint8Array base64/hex (60/60 closed, focused slice now 100%). Branch off main; stacks parallel to PR #3 (DataView BigInt), no expected conflicts. Issue stays in_progress for the remaining clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com

comments.text: TypedArray DefineOwnProperty value-conversion slice landed. Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass

- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass

- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs

- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor buffer-arg ordering slice landed. Added regressions for undefined length modulo behavior and valueOf-triggered detach during byteOffset/length coercion. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. Root cause: TypedArrayCreate helper accepted a custom constructor result whose typed-array length was shorter than the requested length, so from/of wrote past the returned view and produced RangeError instead of the required TypeError. Added a regression covering iterable and array-like from plus of for Uint8Array and BigInt64Array. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; the four custom-ctor Test262 files all passed 1/1 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-bigint-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-short-custom-after.md, and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-bigint-short-custom-after.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-short-custom.md is 665 passed, 71 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray DefineOwnProperty value-conversion slice landed. Root cause: valid typed-array numeric defineProperty needed VM-level IntegerIndexedElementSet semantics so descriptor values are converted with ToNumber/ToBigInt and detach-during-conversion returns true without writing. Added a VM bridge path for typed-array numeric defineProperty, reusing the existing typed-array set path for value writes after descriptor validation, and added a direct regression for numeric conversion plus detach during number and BigInt conversion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/DefineOwnProperty passed 54/54 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-internals-defineownproperty-after-value-set.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-define-value-set.md is 710 passed, 26 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors internals/Set now passes 53/53. Fixed indexed [[Set]] to convert values before detached/index validity checks, return true for aligned detached-buffer semantics, reject Number for BigInt typed arrays, and materialize TypeError for BigInt-to-Number typed-array stores. Full TypedArrayConstructors is now 733/736 files passing; remaining failures are GetOwnProperty enumerate-detached-buffer (Number/BigInt) and OwnPropertyKeys integer-indexes-resizable-array-buffer-fixed.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors is now fully green: 736/736 files and 1442/1442 variants pass at /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-ownkeys-live-length-final.md. comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor buffer-arg ordering slice landed. Root causes: explicit undefined length was treated as length 0 instead of absent, and the ArrayBuffer-backed constructor did not recheck detachment after byteOffset/length ToIndex coercion. Added regressions for undefined length modulo behavior and valueOf-triggered detach during byteOffset/length coercion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 buffer-arg slices passed 54/54 and 52/52 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-buffer-arg.md is 661 passed, 75 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. Root cause: TypedArrayCreate helper accepted a custom constructor result whose typed-array length was shorter than the requested length, so from/of wrote past the returned view and produced RangeError instead of the required TypeError. Added a regression covering iterable and array-like from plus of for Uint8Array and BigInt64Array. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; the four custom-ctor Test262 files all passed 1/1 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-bigint-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-short-custom-after.md, and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-bigint-short-custom-after.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-short-custom.md is 665 passed, 71 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray DefineOwnProperty value-conversion slice landed. Root cause: valid typed-array numeric defineProperty needed VM-level IntegerIndexedElementSet semantics so descriptor values are converted with ToNumber/ToBigInt and detach-during-conversion returns true without writing. Added a VM bridge path for typed-array numeric defineProperty, reusing the existing typed-array set path for value writes after descriptor validation, and added a direct regression for numeric conversion plus detach during number and BigInt conversion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/DefineOwnProperty passed 54/54 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-internals-defineownproperty-after-value-set.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-define-value-set.md is 710 passed, 26 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors internals/Set now passes 53/53. Fixed indexed [[Set]] to convert values before detached/index validity checks, return true for aligned detached-buffer semantics, reject Number for BigInt typed arrays, and materialize TypeError for BigInt-to-Number typed-array stores. Full TypedArrayConstructors is now 733/736 files passing; remaining failures are GetOwnProperty enumerate-detached-buffer (Number/BigInt) and OwnPropertyKeys integer-indexes-resizable-array-buffer-fixed.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors is now fully green: 736/736 files and 1442/1442 variants pass at /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-ownkeys-live-length-final.md. Final slice fixed two roots: loop lowering no longer re-emits TDZ initializers for non-lexical for/for-in/for-of heads (which caused ReferenceError during for-in enumeration in function scopes), and typed-array ownKeys now computes integer-index keys from the live backing-store bounds for fixed resizable ArrayBuffer views. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: added shared live-length validation for %TypedArray% builtin methods so length-tracking views observe current resizable-buffer byte length and already-OOB fixed views fail ValidateTypedArray. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed %TypedArray% mutation resizable-buffer bounds for fill/copyWithin/with and sort compare-callback shrink behavior.
