title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor array-like allocation guard slice landed. Added a regression proving Uint8Array/BigInt64Array oversize array-like construction throws RangeError before index 0 access. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor buffer-arg ordering slice landed. Root causes: explicit undefined length was treated as length 0 instead of absent, and the ArrayBuffer-backed constructor did not recheck detachment after byteOffset/length ToIndex coercion. Added regressions for undefined length modulo behavior and valueOf-triggered detach during byteOffset/length coercion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 buffer-arg slices passed 54/54 and 52/52 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-buffer-arg.md is 661 passed, 75 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. Root cause: TypedArrayCreate helper accepted a custom constructor result whose typed-array length was shorter than the requested length, so from/of wrote past the returned view and produced RangeError instead of the required TypeError. Added a regression covering iterable and array-like from plus of for Uint8Array and BigInt64Array. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; the four custom-ctor Test262 files all passed 1/1 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-bigint-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-short-custom-after.md, and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-bigint-short-custom-after.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-short-custom.md is 665 passed, 71 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray DefineOwnProperty value-conversion slice landed. Root cause: valid typed-array numeric defineProperty needed VM-level IntegerIndexedElementSet semantics so descriptor values are converted with ToNumber/ToBigInt and detach-during-conversion returns true without writing. Added a VM bridge path for typed-array numeric defineProperty, reusing the existing typed-array set path for value writes after descriptor validation, and added a direct regression for numeric conversion plus detach during number and BigInt conversion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/DefineOwnProperty passed 54/54 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-internals-defineownproperty-after-value-set.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-define-value-set.md is 710 passed, 26 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

Added a VM bridge path for typed-array numeric defineProperty, reusing the existing typed-array set path for value writes after descriptor validation, and added a direct regression for numeric conversion plus detach during number and BigInt conversion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/DefineOwnProperty passed 54/54 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-internals-defineownproperty-after-value-set.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-define-value-set.md is 710 passed, 26 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: PR opened: https://github.com/lyngsys/lyng/pull/4 — Uint8Array base64/hex (60/60 closed, focused slice now 100%). Branch off main; stacks parallel to PR #3 (DataView BigInt), no expected conflicts. Issue stays in_progress for the remaining clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor array-like allocation guard slice landed. Root cause: array-like construction collected indexed properties before checking whether AllocateTypedArrayBuffer could fit; excessive lengths timed out and could touch user getters before RangeError. Added a regression proving Uint8Array/BigInt64Array oversize array-like construction throws RangeError before index 0 access. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor buffer-arg ordering slice landed. Root causes: explicit undefined length was treated as length 0 instead of absent, and the ArrayBuffer-backed constructor did not recheck detachment after byteOffset/length ToIndex coercion. Added regressions for undefined length modulo behavior and valueOf-triggered detach during byteOffset/length coercion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 buffer-arg slices passed 54/54 and 52/52 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-buffer-arg.md is 661 passed, 75 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. Root cause: TypedArrayCreate helper accepted a custom constructor result whose typed-array length was shorter than the requested length, so from/of wrote past the returned view and produced RangeError instead of the required TypeError. Added a regression covering iterable and array-like from plus of for Uint8Array and BigInt64Array. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; the four custom-ctor Test262 files all passed 1/1 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-bigint-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-short-custom-after.md, and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-bigint-short-custom-after.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-short-custom.md is 665 passed, 71 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray DefineOwnProperty value-conversion slice landed. Root cause: valid typed-array numeric defineProperty needed VM-level IntegerIndexedElementSet semantics so descriptor values are converted with ToNumber/ToBigInt and detach-during-conversion returns true without writing. Added a VM bridge path for typed-array numeric defineProperty, reusing the existing typed-array set path for value writes after descriptor validation, and added a direct regression for numeric conversion plus detach during number and BigInt conversion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/DefineOwnProperty passed 54/54 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-internals-defineownproperty-after-value-set.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-define-value-set.md is 710 passed, 26 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com

Full TypedArrayConstructors is now 733/736 files passing; remaining failures are GetOwnProperty enumerate-detached-buffer (Number/BigInt) and OwnPropertyKeys integer-indexes-resizable-array-buffer-fixed.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors is now fully green: 736/736 files and 1442/1442 variants pass at /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-ownkeys-live-length-final.md. Final slice fixed two roots: loop lowering no longer re-emits TDZ initializers for non-lexical for/for-in/for-of heads (which caused ReferenceError during for-in enumeration in function scopes), and typed-array ownKeys now computes integer-index keys from the live backing-store bounds for fixed resizable ArrayBuffer views. Verification also covered cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler, -p lyng-js-ops, -p lyng-js-vm, for_in, for_of, normal_for, and script_core_typed_array.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: added shared live-length validation for %TypedArray% builtin methods so length-tracking views observe current resizable-buffer byte length and already-OOB fixed views fail ValidateTypedArray. Access/search and iteration methods now snapshot the live length and route post-coercion indexed misses through typed-array Get semantics. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; Test262 built-ins/TypedArray now 1392/1438 files passing (2768/2860 variants), down from 1364/1438 before this patch. Remaining failures are from/of result resizing, copyWithin/fill/set/slice/subarray/with mutation semantics, species-create resizable destinations, and two compiler lowering duplicate-binding cases.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed %TypedArray% mutation resizable-buffer bounds for fill/copyWithin/with and sort compare-callback shrink behavior. Mutating methods now use the initial live length for index math, recheck current bounds after coercions/callbacks, truncate length-tracking copies, and avoid surfacing backing-store write misses as RangeError where the spec expects TypeError/no write. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; Test262 method slices fill/copyWithin/with and sort/comparefn-shrink all pass; full built-ins/TypedArray is now 1402/1438 files passing (2788/2860 variants).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed the TypedArray species/map resizable-destination cluster plus the compiler loop-class environment issue it exposed. Species-created typed-array results now validate against live length after construction, length-tracking typed-array construction accepts unaligned current byte lengths for resizable buffers, and for-in/of loop lowering refreshes body TDZ environment slots per iteration while skipping nested-function-owned slots. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_lowers; built-ins/TypedArray/prototype/map is now 84/84 files and 168/168 variants passing; full built-ins/TypedArray is now 1416/1438 files and 2816/2860 variants passing. Remaining failures are concentrated in TypedArray.from/of result resizing, prototype.set resizable semantics, slice, and subarray.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed %TypedArray%.from/of result-resize semantics. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed %TypedArray%.prototype.set resizable-buffer semantics. set now coerces offset before the live target validation, rejects already-OOB target/source typed arrays before touching source elements, uses live target/source lengths for the initial range checks, and routes each target write through typed-array Set semantics so mid-iteration shrinks skip invalid writes after value conversion. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed %TypedArray%.prototype.slice resizable source revalidation. slice now uses the live initial length for argument normalization, preserves the original requested result length, then re-witnesses the source after argument coercion/species construction and copies only the still-live range; fixed views that become OOB throw TypeError, while length-tracking shrink leaves the rest of the result zero-filled. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; built-ins/TypedArray/prototype/slice is now 91/91 files and 182/182 variants passing; full built-ins/TypedArray is now 1433/1438 files and 2850/2860 variants passing. Remaining failures are all prototype.subarray.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: completed the remaining %TypedArray%.prototype.subarray resizable/species cluster. subarray now computes source length from the live witness length (using 0 for OOB sources), preserves the original byteOffset calculation, and passes two species constructor arguments for auto-length views when end is omitted so subarray results remain length-tracking. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; built-ins/TypedArray/prototype/subarray is 67/67 files and 134/134 variants passing; full built-ins/TypedArray is now 1438/1438 files and 2860/2860 variants passing.

title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_lowers; built-ins/TypedArray/prototype/map is now 84/84 files and 168/168 variants passing; full built-ins/TypedArray is now 1416/1438 files and 2816/2860 variants passing. Remaining failures are concentrated in TypedArray.from/of result resizing, prototype.set resizable semantics, slice, and subarray.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Progress: fixed %TypedArray%.from/of result-resize semantics. The fill loops now use the existing Set helper instead of direct storage writes, so mapper/value conversion can detach or resize the custom constructor result and out-of-bounds numeric writes are ignored per typed-array Set semantics. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array; focused from/of files passed; built-ins/TypedArray/from is 21/21 files and 42/42 variants passing; built-ins/TypedArray/of is 8/8 files and 16/16 variants passing; full built-ins/TypedArray is now 1423/1438 files and 2830/2860 variants passing. title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.text: PR opened: https://github.com/lyngsys/lyng/pull/4 — Uint8Array base64/hex (60/60 closed, focused slice now 100%). Branch off main; stacks parallel to PR #3 (DataView BigInt), no expected conflicts. Issue stays in_progress for the remaining clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor array-like allocation guard slice landed. Root cause: array-like construction collected indexed properties before checking whether AllocateTypedArrayBuffer could fit; excessive lengths timed out and could touch user getters before RangeError. Added a regression proving Uint8Array/BigInt64Array oversize array-like construction throws RangeError before index 0 access. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 object-arg slices passed 28/28 and 31/31 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-object-arg-after-oversize.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-object-arg-after-oversize.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-oversize.md is 653 passed, 83 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray constructor buffer-arg ordering slice landed. Root causes: explicit undefined length was treated as length 0 instead of absent, and the ArrayBuffer-backed constructor did not recheck detachment after byteOffset/length ToIndex coercion. Added regressions for undefined length modulo behavior and valueOf-triggered detach during byteOffset/length coercion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 buffer-arg slices passed 54/54 and 52/52 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-ctors-bigint-buffer-arg-after-detach-order.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-buffer-arg.md is 661 passed, 75 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. Root cause: TypedArrayCreate helper accepted a custom constructor result whose typed-array length was shorter than the requested length, so from/of wrote past the returned view and produced RangeError instead of the required TypeError. Added a regression covering iterable and array-like from plus of for Uint8Array and BigInt64Array. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; the four custom-ctor Test262 files all passed 1/1 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-from-bigint-short-custom-after.md, /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-short-custom-after.md, and /tmp/lyng-js-ta-of-bigint-short-custom-after.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-short-custom.md is 665 passed, 71 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray DefineOwnProperty value-conversion slice landed. Root cause: valid typed-array numeric defineProperty needed VM-level IntegerIndexedElementSet semantics so descriptor values are converted with ToNumber/ToBigInt and detach-during-conversion returns true without writing. Added a VM bridge path for typed-array numeric defineProperty, reusing the existing typed-array set path for value writes after descriptor validation, and added a direct regression for numeric conversion plus detach during number and BigInt conversion. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/DefineOwnProperty passed 54/54 at /tmp/lyng-js-ta-internals-defineownproperty-after-value-set.md; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-define-value-set.md is 710 passed, 26 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors internals/Set now passes 53/53. Fixed indexed [[Set]] to convert values before detached/index validity checks, return true for aligned detached-buffer semantics, reject Number for BigInt typed arrays, and materialize TypeError for BigInt-to-Number typed-array stores. Full TypedArrayConstructors is now 733/736 files passing; remaining failures are GetOwnProperty enumerate-detached-buffer (Number/BigInt) and OwnPropertyKeys integer-indexes-resizable-array-buffer-fixed.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArrayConstructors is now fully green: 736/736 files and 1442/1442 variants pass at /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-ownkeys-live-length-final.md. Final slice fixed two roots: loop lowering no longer re-emits TDZ initializers for non-lexical for/for-in/for-of heads (which caused ReferenceError during for-in enumeration in function scopes), and typed-array ownKeys now computes integer-index keys from the live backing-store bounds for fixed resizable ArrayBuffer views. title: Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
Eliminate built-ins harness, bootstrap, and final report skips
description:
Eliminate remaining built-ins selected skips and reporting tails that are not owned by one semantic built-in family.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.

Skip clusters in scope:
- Bootstrap gaps: Number 6, String 6, Array 3, Object.getOwnPropertyNames 3, JSON 2, Math 2, and single-case Object/JSON/eval bootstrap needs.
- Harness capability: `$262.agent` multi-agent harness 59.
- Host/runtime policy: host runs with `[[CanBlock]]` true 2.
- Runtime safety skips not absorbed by binary-data work, if any remain.
- Final built-ins report reconciliation after semantic child issues land.

Relevant source owners: spec bootstrap in `crates/lyng-js/builtins`, harness extensions and skip decisions in `tools/lyng-js-test262`, host policy in `crates/lyng-js/host`/`env`, and checked-in reports under `reports/js/lyng-js` only when explicitly regenerating report artifacts.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes rerunning `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12` after other child issues land.
- Built-ins skip count is `0` except for cases removed from selection by explicit project policy; no ECMA-262 built-ins gap is hidden in the manifest.
- `$262.agent`, bootstrap, host-policy, and runtime-safety skips are either implemented or documented as out of scope with explicit manifest/report alignment.
- `dcat list <epic-id>` and the final built-ins report agree on remaining work before the epic can move to review.

title: Close Array and Array.prototype built-in failures
Close binary-data built-in failures and safety skips
description:
Close TypedArray, ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, DataView, and binary text-conversion built-ins gaps from the April 24, 2026 built-ins baseline.

Baseline evidence:
- Full slice command: `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md -j 12`
- Built-ins baseline: 23,388 selected, 16,614 passed, 4,825 failed, 1,949 skipped.
- Raw failure inventory shows TypedArrayConstructors 110, DataView 78, TypedArray 11, Uint8Array base64/hex 60, ArrayBuffer 2, SharedArrayBuffer 2.
- Skips include resizable-arraybuffer 450, arraybuffer-transfer 41, oversize Array.prototype.slice guard 4, and oversize ArrayBuffer allocation guard 2.

Major clusters:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/internals`: 69 failures.
- DataView BigInt/float16/get/set clusters: 78 failures.
- `Uint8Array.fromBase64/fromHex`, `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64/setFromHex`, `toBase64`, and `toHex`: 60 failures.
- Large allocation and length-excessive tests currently time out or abort.

Example failures:
- `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ctors/object-arg/length-excessive-throws.js: timeout after 1.0s`
- `built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64/results.js: runtime error: TypeError`
- `built-ins/DataView/prototype/getBigInt64/` cluster failures from the raw list.

Likely owner areas: typed-array and DataView builtins in `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs`, backing-store model in `crates/lyng-js/objects`, shared-memory ops, and `docs/lyng-js/shared-memory-and-backing-stores.md` if ownership/status changes.
acceptance:
- Focused verification includes `built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors`, `built-ins/TypedArray`, `built-ins/DataView`, `built-ins/Uint8Array`, `built-ins/ArrayBuffer`, and `built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer` reports.
- Focused binary-data reports have `Failed: 0`, `Skipped: 0`, and `Panicked: 0` for selected tests.
- Oversize allocation and excessive-length cases fail fast with typed guest-visible completions rather than process aborts or timeouts.
- Direct tests cover changed backing-store, DataView, typed-array, resizable/transfer, and base64/hex behavior.
closed_reason: Completed, verified, and approved for closure.

Second cluster landed: Uint8Array base64/hex (Stage 4 proposal-arraybuffer-base64).

Test262 deltas (focused slice):
- built-ins/Uint8Array: 60 failed -> 0 failed (-60, all 6 methods passing 100%)
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/fromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/setFromHex: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toBase64: all tests pass
- built-ins/Uint8Array/prototype/toHex: all tests pass

Implementation:
- 6 new builtin function ids (3554-3559) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- New module crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/typed_arrays/base64_hex.rs (~700 lines) with:
  - decode_base64 mirroring the proposal's FromBase64 abstract op (alphabet, lastChunkHandling, optional maxLength).
  - decode_hex mirroring FromHex with odd-length and max-length stop semantics.
  - encode_base64 (standard + base64url tables, omitPadding option).
  - encode_hex (lowercase output).
  - 6 dispatch handlers performing ValidateUint8Array first per spec, then string/options/detached checks in spec order.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins, PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables.
- Static fromBase64/fromHex install on Uint8Array constructor; setFromBase64/setFromHex/toBase64/toHex on Uint8Array.prototype, all (writable, !enumerable, configurable).

Out of scope for this PR (still on lyng-38iz):
- DataView.prototype.getFloat16/setFloat16 (62 failures; needs IEEE-754 half-precision conversion since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16).
- TypedArrayConstructors/internals (~110 failures).
- Resizable ArrayBuffer skips, ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer skips.
- Oversize allocation/excessive-length runtime guards.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text:
Third cluster landed on PR #4: DataView Float16 (getFloat16, setFloat16).

Test262 deltas (focused):
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/getFloat16: 40/40 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView/prototype/setFloat16: 44/44 runnable pass
- built-ins/DataView whole-slice (still missing PR #3 BigInt fixes): 94 fail (was 156 pre-Float16; the 92 BigInt failures from PR #3 territory remain). When both PR #3 and PR #4 merge: ~2 unrelated edge cases.

Implementation:
- 2 new builtin function ids (3560-3561) in crates/lyng-js/types/src/builtin_ids.rs
- IEEE-754 binary16 conversion implemented manually in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/binary_data/data_view.rs since stable Rust 1.93 still lacks f16:
  - float16_bits_to_f64: handles zero/sign, subnormal renormalization (find leading bit, shift up, rebias), Infinity/NaN preservation, normal rebiasing.
  - f64_to_float16_bits: round-to-nearest-ties-even via round_shift_signif helper. Special-cases NaN/Infinity, normal/subnormal output decisions (overflow -> sign-preserved Infinity, underflow -> sign-preserved zero), mantissa overflow on round-up that promotes subnormal -> normal or normal -> next exponent.
  - 5 inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests verify the tricky cases from byteConversionValues.js: zeros and specials, normal round-trips, ties-to-even at 2049/2051, subnormal round-to-even at 2.98e-8/half-the-smallest, max-finite 65504 vs overflow at 65520, and the smallest representable subnormal.
- Wired through BinaryDataFamilyBuiltins/PublicRealmBuiltins, install/lookup/descriptor/metadata tables in the same pattern as the BigInt fixes.

Now folded into PR #4 (originally Uint8Array base64/hex). Updated title to cover both clusters.

comments.text: TypedArray.from/of custom-constructor short-result slice landed. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: TypedArray numeric-index internal-method slice landed. Root cause: typed-array exotic handling only recognized PropertyKey::Index, so canonical numeric strings such as 1.1, -0, -1, and out-of-bounds numeric keys fell through to ordinary prototype/define/delete behavior. Added shared canonical numeric-index handling for ordinary get/getOwnProperty/has/define/delete, with VM get preserving the no-prototype path. Added regressions for get/has prototype suppression, define invalid numeric-index rejection, and detached delete returning true. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-ops passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests script_core_typed_array passed; Test262 internals/Get, internals/HasProperty, and internals/Delete all passed 100%; internals/DefineOwnProperty improved to 46/54 files passing with only value-conversion cases remaining; full TypedArrayConstructors report /tmp/lyng-js-typedarrayconstructors-after-numeric-keys.md is 702 passed, 34 failed, 0 skipped, 0 panicked.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Per-test timing instrumentation is now in the Test262 report. Full run report: reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md. Current slowest files are URI exhaustive tests: built-ins/decodeURIComponent/S15.1.3.2_A2.5_T1.js at ~32.680s total / ~16.437s slowest variant in the full -j12 run, and built-ins/decodeURI/S15.1.3.1_A2.5_T1.js at ~31.246s total / ~15.821s slowest variant. Targeted -j1 decodeURIComponent run measures ~8s per variant, so the URI path is a real hotspot and full-suite timings also reflect CPU contention. Next group is generated RegExp CharacterClassEscapes/property-escapes tests around ~2.0-2.3s per variant. Acceptance for the next optimization pass: improve these hot paths without increasing harness timeouts, keep timing report output, and verify with targeted Test262 slices plus relevant Rust tests.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Optimization pass completed and full Test262 report regenerated at reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md. Full run is now 124.2s at -j12, down from 165.7s initial timed report and 130.8s after the first pass, with panicked files still 0 and pass/fail improving from 48910/754 to 48911/753. Main fixes: primitive string index/length reads avoid ToObject wrapper allocation, one-code-unit Latin1 strings are cached, small string allocations avoid temporary Vecs, string concatenation appends directly, Smi binary operations use a VM fast path, String.fromCharCode/fromCodePoint fast-path Smi arguments, and global StoreGlobal/AssignGlobal now use named-property store feedback. Final slowest files are decodeURI at 9.988s total / 5.031s slowest variant and decodeURIComponent at 9.728s / 4.869s; next remaining group is generated RegExp CharacterClassEscapes/property-escapes around 2.3-2.4s per variant. Focused verification passed: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests phase6_string_text_builtins_cover_missing_core_methods; cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler compile_script_assigns_named_store_feedback_to_global_stores; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm global_property_store_ic_caches_global_object_data_property; targeted URI and RegExp Test262 filters. Full cargo test -p lyng-js-vm --lib still has two named tests failing; the Smi fast path was disabled during a check and those failures remained, so they do not appear to be caused by this fast path.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Checkpoint before second optimization commit: added guarded dense engine-array indexed stores and Function.apply dense-array argument collection, plus RegExp fast paths for simple character-class scans and anchored generated runs. Root-cause isolation found an array-store fast-path regression through typed-array prototypes; fixed by making the prototype-chain guard bail on typed-array objects. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-objects fast (4 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-vm evaluate_script_array_index_assignment (2 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-vm evaluate_script_function_apply_observes_array_prototype_getter (1 passed); git diff --check; targeted typed-array Test262 Set slices passed 2/2 variants each; regenerated full Test262 report completed in 95.8s with 48,912 passed files / 752 failed files / 0 panics, vs committed checkpoint 124.2s with 48,911 / 753. Remaining hotspots: decodeURI/decodeURIComponent generated tests (~8.6s/~8.4s total) and RegExp Unicode property escape generated tests still dominate the middle slowdown.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Checkpoint: isolated generated RegExp property optimization. Added fast paths for simple Unicode property patterns in the generated suite: Any, ASCII, ASCII_Hex_Digit/AHex, Bidi_Control/Bidi_C, plus impossible \P{Any} cases. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-objects regexp passed 7/7; cargo build --release -p lyng-js-test262 passed; generated RegExp property slice improved from 82.0s baseline to 67.5s with 938/938 variants passing and 0 panics. A full report run completed with no panics but was not committed because staging timeout-sensitive counts regressed and wall time was noisy (112.3s vs previous 95.8s) while built-ins counts remained unchanged.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Checkpoint: optimized string + string concatenation in Vm::add_values after profiling the generated RegExp property slice. Sampled worker stack showed the slow cluster dominated by add_values/alloc_utf16_string from buildString result += chunks, with regexp matching much smaller. Added a direct string-ref concat path that preserves ToPrimitive ordering and avoids the intermediate Vec<u16> plus second encoding scan for string+string. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm string passed 16/16; cargo build --release -p lyng-js-test262 passed; built-ins/RegExp/property-escapes/generated passed 938/938 in 60.7s, down from 67.5s after the RegExp-only checkpoint and 82.0s baseline. The two top URI files both passed in 3.7s. Full cargo test -p lyng-js-vm still has two pre-existing failures on HEAD: named_generator_expression_self_binding_ignores_sloppy_reassignment and named_property_load_ic_grows_polymorphic_and_then_megamorphic.

Final slowest files are decodeURI at 9.988s total / 5.031s slowest variant and decodeURIComponent at 9.728s / 4.869s; next remaining group is generated RegExp CharacterClassEscapes/property-escapes around 2.3-2.4s per variant. Focused verification passed: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests phase6_string_text_builtins_cover_missing_core_methods; cargo test -p lyng-js-compiler compile_script_assigns_named_store_feedback_to_global_stores; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm global_property_store_ic_caches_global_object_data_property; targeted URI and RegExp Test262 filters. Full cargo test -p lyng-js-vm --lib still has two named tests failing; the Smi fast path was disabled during a check and those failures remained, so they do not appear to be caused by this fast path.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Checkpoint before second optimization commit: added guarded dense engine-array indexed stores and Function.apply dense-array argument collection, plus RegExp fast paths for simple character-class scans and anchored generated runs. Root-cause isolation found an array-store fast-path regression through typed-array prototypes; fixed by making the prototype-chain guard bail on typed-array objects. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-objects fast (4 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-vm evaluate_script_array_index_assignment (2 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-vm evaluate_script_function_apply_observes_array_prototype_getter (1 passed); git diff --check; targeted typed-array Test262 Set slices passed 2/2 variants each; regenerated full Test262 report completed in 95.8s with 48,912 passed files / 752 failed files / 0 panics, vs committed checkpoint 124.2s with 48,911 / 753. Remaining hotspots: decodeURI/decodeURIComponent generated tests (~8.6s/~8.4s total) and RegExp Unicode property escape generated tests still dominate the middle slowdown.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Checkpoint: isolated generated RegExp property optimization. Added fast paths for simple Unicode property patterns in the generated suite: Any, ASCII, ASCII_Hex_Digit/AHex, Bidi_Control/Bidi_C, plus impossible \P{Any} cases. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-objects regexp passed 7/7; cargo build --release -p lyng-js-test262 passed; generated RegExp property slice improved from 82.0s baseline to 67.5s with 938/938 variants passing and 0 panics. A full report run completed with no panics but was not committed because staging timeout-sensitive counts regressed and wall time was noisy (112.3s vs previous 95.8s) while built-ins counts remained unchanged.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Checkpoint: optimized string + string concatenation in Vm::add_values after profiling the generated RegExp property slice. Sampled worker stack showed the slow cluster dominated by add_values/alloc_utf16_string from buildString result += chunks, with regexp matching much smaller. Added a direct string-ref concat path that preserves ToPrimitive ordering and avoids the intermediate Vec<u16> plus second encoding scan for string+string. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-vm string passed 16/16; cargo build --release -p lyng-js-test262 passed; built-ins/RegExp/property-escapes/generated passed 938/938 in 60.7s, down from 67.5s after the RegExp-only checkpoint and 82.0s baseline. The two top URI files both passed in 3.7s. Full cargo test -p lyng-js-vm still has two pre-existing failures on HEAD: named_generator_expression_self_binding_ignores_sloppy_reassignment and named_property_load_ic_grows_polymorphic_and_then_megamorphic.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Full report check after RegExp and concat checkpoints: target/release/lyng-js-test262 --report reports/js/lyng-js/test262.md -j 12 completed with 0 panics, but wall time was 123.2s and staging timeout-sensitive counts regressed to 1279 passed / 198 failed files (2428 / 343 variants). Built-ins and language counts stayed flat at 22890/498 and 23577/55, so the regression is not attributable to the optimized built-ins slices. The generated report was restored and not committed because it would encode noisy timeout churn rather than a stable conformance delta.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: String-use review found several runtime paths converting JS strings through Rust String via value_to_string_text/string_ref_text. Because UTF-16 decoding is lossy for lone surrogates, String.prototype.concat, Array.prototype.join, TypedArray.prototype.join separators, Array object-to-primitive fallback, and String.raw were doing unnecessary intermediate text/vector allocation and could corrupt exact code units. Patch switches those paths to direct runtime UTF-16 code-unit append helpers and adds VM regressions for concat/join preservation. Remaining notable String push_str use is Function constructor source building; left as design follow-up because the parser currently requires Rust String source text.

Primary owner crates and tools: `crates/lyng-js/builtins`, `crates/lyng-js/ops`, `crates/lyng-js/objects`, `crates/lyng-js/env`, `tools/lyng-js-test262`. Keep fixes in the owning semantic layer rather than duplicating abstract operations inside builtins.

description:
Own the full Test262 `built-ins` pass-rate gap for Lyng JS, including both runnable failures and selected skipped cases. Skips are in scope because the selected pass rate counts them against completion.

- `cargo run --release -p lyng-js-test262 -- --filter built-ins --report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins-list.md --list-failures -j 12` produced the raw 4,825-failure inventory used for clustering.

Scope:
- In scope: ECMA-262 built-ins, non-Intl Temporal built-ins, Stage 3+ built-in feature skips selected by the current runner policy, built-ins harness/bootstrap/runtime-policy skips that count against selected pass rate.
- Out of scope: ECMA-402 `intl402/*`, below-Stage-3 proposal exclusions, and web/Node host APIs.

Primary owner crates and tools: `crates/lyng-js/builtins`, `crates/lyng-js/ops`, `crates/lyng-js/objects`, `crates/lyng-js/env`, `tools/lyng-js-test262`. Keep fixes in the owning semantic layer rather than duplicating abstract operations inside builtins.
acceptance:

Focused engine regressions for PlainDate fast-path conversion and accessor avoidance pass locally, so this should be tracked separately as Test262 harness helper parity rather than hidden inside the Temporal date-time difference implementation work.

title: Restore Temporal Test262 helper parity for PlainDateTime fast-path checks
description: The local Test262 Temporal helper shim in tools/lyng-js-test262/src/temporal_helpers.js is missing upstream-style helper coverage used by Temporal tests, specifically TemporalHelpers.checkToTemporalPlainDateTimeFastPath. As a result, built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime/prototype/since/argument-plaindate.js fails with runtime TypeError before it reaches engine behavior. This can make Test262 reports look like engine failures and can also hide real engine flaws if helper shims diverge or weaken assertions. Preserve assertion strength: prefer using upstream helper behavior directly where possible, or add narrowly scoped parity with tests that prove getters are not called and assertions still fail when expected.
acceptance: 1. The Temporal Test262 harness provides TemporalHelpers.checkToTemporalPlainDateTimeFastPath with upstream-equivalent observable behavior. 2. The helper does not weaken or bypass assertions; it verifies internal-slot fast paths by installing observable getters and expecting no getter activity. 3. built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime/prototype/since/argument-plaindate.js runs past helper setup and reflects engine behavior rather than missing-helper TypeError. 4. Add focused harness tests or runner coverage showing missing helpers fail loudly and the restored helper catches accessor reads.

Close Temporal date-time difference and rounding tail
description: Fresh built-ins report /tmp/lyng-js-builtins.md shows remaining Temporal PlainDateTime, PlainDate, PlainYearMonth, and Duration.prototype.total failures. Major symptoms include unexpected RangeError/Test262Error in since()/until(), leap-second input, round-cross-unit-boundary cases, largestUnit defaults, order-of-operations tests, withPlainTime object/string conversions, and exact mathematical Duration.total precision cases.
acceptance: Focused filters for built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime, built-ins/Temporal/PlainDate, built-ins/Temporal/PlainYearMonth, and built-ins/Temporal/Duration/prototype/total covering the reported failures pass with 0 failures, 0 skips, and 0 panics. Full built-ins report shows the corresponding failure clusters removed or materially reduced without exclusions.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Harness finding while investigating PlainDateTime argument-plaindate: the failing Test262 file built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime/prototype/since/argument-plaindate.js currently reports runtime TypeError before it reaches Temporal engine behavior because the local helper shim tools/lyng-js-test262/src/temporal_helpers.js does not define TemporalHelpers.checkToTemporalPlainDateTimeFastPath. Focused engine regressions for PlainDate fast-path conversion and accessor avoidance pass locally, so this should be tracked separately as Test262 harness helper parity rather than hidden inside the Temporal date-time difference implementation work.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implementation verification update: engine-side Temporal date/datetime/year-month/duration tail is complete for the targeted slices. Fresh results: PlainDate since 84/84 and until 83/83; ZonedDateTime since 99/99 and until 97/97; Duration.prototype.total 76/76. PlainDateTime since/until are 91/92 and 94/95 with only missing helper TypeErrors in argument-plaindate, tracked by lyng-4az3. PlainYearMonth is 495/505 with only missing TemporalHelpers.ISO PlainYearMonth string-list TypeErrors in argument-string files, tracked separately by lyng-19kr. Rust focused suites for temporal_duration, temporal_plain_year_month, temporal_zoned_date_time, temporal_plain_date_time_difference, temporal_plain_date_since, and temporal_plain_date_difference pass.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implementation update: restored Temporal.PlainDateTime.prototype.withPlainTime by assigning it a non-aliasing builtin id (it had collided with ArrayBuffer.prototype.resize), aligned PlainDateTime.prototype.with field/order/options behavior with the PlainDate pattern, restricted PlainDateTime.round to day-or-smaller rounding units, and added representable-range checks after PlainDateTime arithmetic/string rounding. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-types passed 28/28; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests temporal_plain_date_time passed 22/22; Test262 built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime/prototype/withPlainTime passed 37 files / 74 variants; with passed 30 files / 60 variants; round passed 45 files / 90 variants; full built-ins/Temporal/PlainDateTime passed 767 files / 1534 variants; Duration.prototype.total stayed clean at 76 files / 152 variants.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented the variable-length typed-array [[PreventExtensions]] rule in the object runtime. RAB-backed typed-array views now make [[PreventExtensions]] return false even when currently zero-length; growable SharedArrayBuffer views return false only for length-tracking views. title: Burn down Test262 built-ins failures and skips to 100%
Research native backend options and dependency policy
description:
Research native backend options for a future Lyng JS JIT without adding a backend dependency yet. Compare realistic options such as Cranelift, dynasm-style assembly emission, custom machine-code emission, and interpreter-threaded or bytecode-specialization alternatives.

The research must respect repo policy: dependency growth is a design decision, hot-path discipline matters, and native execution must be feature-gated and platform-aware. Initial platform evidence should cover the current macOS/aarch64 development target and note x86_64 implications.
acceptance:
A written recommendation compares backend options by dependency cost, platform support, codegen control, safepoint/stack-map support, callout integration, compile latency, maintenance risk, and fit with Lyng JS crate boundaries.
No backend dependency is added without a separate approved implementation issue.
The recommended first implementation path, if any, is scoped as a future child issue or follow-up epic.

title: Research native backend options and dependency policy
Spike non-default Cranelift baseline backend
description: Prototype a future Lyng JS native baseline backend using Cranelift only after the JIT-readiness substrate has explicit tiering, safepoint/root-map, callout, reporting, and feature-gating contracts. This issue must not add Cranelift to the default workspace dependency set. The spike should be non-default, either behind a Cargo feature or isolated prototype crate, and should treat macOS/aarch64 plus x86_64 execution-memory constraints as acceptance blockers before executable code lands.
acceptance:
- Cranelift is not a default workspace dependency and cannot affect normal builds unless an explicit non-default feature/prototype target is selected.
- The spike lowers only a tiny straight-line bytecode subset such as Smi arithmetic and return.
- Slow paths go through a documented VM/runtime callout ABI instead of reaching across crate internals.
- Safepoint/root-map policy is documented and tested; if the first subset forbids GC-triggering calls, that restriction is explicit and enforced.
- macOS/aarch64 and x86_64 executable-memory feasibility notes are recorded before native code is enabled.
- Benchmark/report output compares interpreter, bytecode-specialized, and native-spike compile/run costs.

title: Research native backend options and dependency policy
Define VM tiering and hotness-state contract
description:
Define the interpreter-side tiering contract needed before JIT work can start. The key design is per-`CodeRef` state for hotness, tier eligibility, tier status, invalidation, and future native-code attachment, without compiling or executing native code.

Relevant code: `crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm.rs`, `crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/feedback.rs`, `crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/install.rs`, `crates/lyng-js/vm/src/installed.rs`, and `crates/lyng-js/vm/src/frame.rs`.

The contract must preserve shared feedback semantics for closures that share one `CodeRef` and must keep guest-visible execution identical to interpreter-only execution.
acceptance:
The design specifies tier states, hotness counters, transition triggers, invalidation behavior, and where the state lives.
No native-code backend is introduced by this task.
Focused VM tests or design examples cover shared `CodeRef` feedback, lazy feedback allocation, and opt-in tier eligibility without changing script results.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented interpreter-side per-CodeRef tiering contract. Added TierStatus/TieringSnapshot plus explicit set_tier_eligible and invalidate_tier_state APIs; tier state is installed beside each runtime code record, feedback-site execution and backward jumps update hotness only for eligible code, and invalidation clears hotness/native-generation state without adding a backend. Documented the VM tiering contract in docs/lyng-js/bytecode-and-vm.md. Added focused VM tests for opt-in eligibility with lazy feedback allocation, shared CodeRef tiering hotness across closures, loop/backedge readiness, and invalidation. Verification: red run cargo test -p lyng-js-vm tiering_hotness -- --nocapture failed before implementation with missing TierStatus/tiering APIs; cargo test -p lyng-js-vm passed 308 unit tests and 0 doctests; cargo fmt --all --check exited 0; git diff --check exited 0. Wider guard cargo test -p lyng-js-tests still has pre-existing failures in execution_semantics::classes::phase6_derived_constructors_bind_this_once_and_initialize_instance_elements and execution_semantics::optional_chaining::delete_super_property_throws_reference_error with MissingEnvironment(EnvironmentRef(3)); both failures reproduce unchanged at baseline commit 647e709.
